name: ax
description: The pretty much "official" DSPy framework for Typescript

commit.message: Tighten AxAgent RLM runtime and context handling
chore: release v19.0.15
Fix adaptive replay tests and lint
chore: release v19.0.14
Add adaptive action replay for AxAgent RLM
fix: optimize discovery prompts for axagent
fix: make llm use batch functions
chore: release v19.0.13
feat: implement patchGlobals method for AxCodeSession and update related functionality
chore: release v19.0.12
feat: add RLM Discovery example with writing coach and analytics tools
fix: update model names and costs for Google Gemini configurations

feat: add inputUpdateCallback for dynamic input updates during actor turns

- Introduced inputUpdateCallback to allow host applications to patch inputs before each Actor turn.
- Updated documentation to reflect the new callback functionality and its usage.
- Added tests to ensure correct behavior of inputUpdateCallback, including handling of unknown keys and no-op scenarios.
chore: release v19.0.11
fix: update schema validation to allow arbitrary JSON objects in structured outputs
feat: add local field support to keep shared fields available in parent agents

fix: don't throw on bare object schemas in Anthropic tool parameters (#494)

MCP servers commonly define tool parameters as `{ type: 'object' }` with
no properties, which caused cleanSchemaForAnthropic to throw before the
existing fallback code (lines 296-313) could add dummy properties.

The throw was unnecessary because createChatReq already handles bare
object schemas by injecting a dummy property after cleanSchemaForAnthropic
runs. By returning early instead of throwing, we let that existing
fallback do its job, fixing compatibility with MCP tool schemas.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

tree 9f9987ad17526639f010920a8841fd9a8f689ff6
parent 74c46e5667db8f3125dde57fef17148a86acfc12
author Amit <3540115+amitdeshmukh@users.noreply.github.com> 1772353666 +0530
committer GitHub <noreply@github.com> 1772353666 -0800

fix: don't throw on bare object schemas in Anthropic tool parameters (#494)

MCP servers commonly define tool parameters as `{ type: 'object' }` with
no properties, which caused cleanSchemaForAnthropic to throw before the
existing fallback code (lines 296-313) could add dummy properties.

The throw was unnecessary because createChatReq already handles bare
object schemas by injecting a dummy property after cleanSchemaForAnthropic
runs. By returning early instead of throwing, we let that existing
fallback do its job, fixing compatibility with MCP tool schemas.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message: chore: release v19.0.10
feat: implement session auto-recovery after timeout and improve error handling
chore: release v19.0.9
feat: enhance error handling by providing focused source context for runtime errors
chore: release v19.0.8

feat: enhance error formatting and template rendering

- Updated `_formatCodeError` to include line offset adjustment for async functions, improving stack trace accuracy.
- Modified build scripts in `package.json` to include template building and testing.
- Refactored actor and responder definitions to utilize a new template rendering system, improving maintainability and readability.
- Added comprehensive tests for the new template engine and integration with existing prompt structures.
- Introduced new markdown templates for formatting rules and function call instructions, ensuring clarity in output requirements.
- Implemented partial templates to reduce redundancy and improve consistency across documentation.
chore: release v19.0.7
refactor: remove Promise from async function signatures and improve error formatting
chore: release v19.0.6
fix: build fix
chore: release v19.0.5
feat: enhance context field handling and improve type normalization in RLM
chore: release v19.0.4
refactor: rename maxLlmCalls to maxSubAgentCalls for consistency across documentation and code
chore: release v19.0.3

Refactor AxAgent and related types for improved context handling and function registration

- Updated AxAgentFunction to AxFunction for consistency across the codebase.
- Enhanced contextFields to support both string and object formats, allowing for promptMaxChars configuration.
- Refactored context management logic in AxAgent to streamline context extraction and validation.
- Improved error handling for contextFields and agent functions.
- Updated documentation and examples to reflect changes in contextFields and function types.
- Adjusted rendering functions to accommodate new function signatures and descriptions.
chore: release v19.0.2
feat: add self-registration prevention for child agents and update documentation references
chore: release v19.0.1
feat: update agent function structure to use object notation for functions and agents
chore: release v19.0.0
chore: release v18.0.14

feat: enhance AxAgent with agent function management and sharing capabilities

- Introduced AxAgentFunction type for defining agent functions with parameters and return schemas.
- Updated AxAgentOptions to include structured configuration for agents, fields, and functions.
- Implemented methods for excluding shared agents and functions from parent agents.
- Enhanced agent function propagation to child agents and globally shared functions.
- Refactored existing code to accommodate new agent function structure and improve organization.
- Updated documentation and examples to reflect changes in agent function usage.
chore: release v18.0.13
feat: Add support for shared fields and agents in AxAgent, enhancing agent hierarchy data passing
chore: release v18.0.12
feat: Add ai parameter to wrapFunction and related methods in AxAgent for enhanced functionality
chore: release v18.0.11
feat: Enhance shared fields handling in AxAgent and add new tests for parameter scoping
chore: release v18.0.10
feat: Add toInputJSONSchema method and related tests for AxSignature and agent function parameters
chore: release v18.0.9
feat: Add support for shared fields in AxAgent and context management
feat: Enhance context management with updated tombstoning options and new example

feat: Implement semantic context management in AxAgent

- Introduced AxContextManagementConfig for managing action log entries.
- Added error pruning, hindsight evaluation, tombstoning, and state inspection features.
- Updated AxAgent to utilize context management options, replacing deprecated trajectoryPruning.
- Enhanced action log handling with new buildActionLog and manageContext functions.
- Created contextManager module with utility functions for evaluating action log entries.
- Added tests for context management functionalities, ensuring correct behavior of pruning and evaluation.
- Updated documentation to reflect new context management features and configuration options.
chore: release v18.0.8
feat(runtime): add consecutive execution error cutoff and enhance error handling in AxJSRuntime
chore: release v18.0.7
feat(worker): add tests for variable persistence across async calls and enhance axWorkerRuntime with top-level declaration extraction
chore: release v18.0.6

feat(worker): enhance axWorkerRuntime and getWorkerSource with improved serialization handling and bundler polyfills

- Updated axWorkerRuntime to ensure compatibility with ESM workers and Node 22.3+ using process.getBuiltinModule.
- Added tests to validate serialized function behavior, ensuring no bare require or import calls are present.
- Enhanced getWorkerSource to prepend polyfills for bundler-injected helpers like __name.
- Improved documentation for serialization rules and polyfill management.
- Refined error handling and output capturing in worker context.
- Updated rlm prompt guidance for better clarity on context handling and code execution strategies.

fix(worker): use bundler-safe require access in serialized runtime

esbuild/tsup replaces bare `require` and `typeof require` with
module-scope polyfill variables during minification. Since
axWorkerRuntime is serialized via .toString() and evaluated in an
isolated worker context, those polyfill variables don't exist,
causing the worker to crash silently and all executions to time out.

Replace bare require with globalThis['require'] which esbuild does
not transform. Add protective comments and regression tests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
chore: release v18.0.5

feat(worker): implement axWorkerRuntime for improved worker source management

- Introduced axWorkerRuntime to encapsulate worker runtime logic and configuration.
- Added tests for axWorkerRuntime to ensure proper error handling when postMessage is unavailable.
- Refactored getWorkerSource to utilize axWorkerRuntime, simplifying worker initialization.
- Enhanced async function handling and error serialization within the worker context.
chore: release v18.0.4
feat: Implement getUsageInstructions method in AxCodeRuntime and update related usages across multiple files for consistency

fix: Migrate from nested `rlm` object to top-level properties for context fields, runtime, and other options across multiple agents and examples. Update documentation and examples to reflect the new structure, ensuring clarity in agent definitions and improving consistency in code organization.
chore: release v18.0.3

feat: Enhance AxAgent with recursion options and action description logging

- Introduced AxAgentRecursionOptions to manage recursion depth and options for sub-agent calls.
- Updated AxAgent to handle actor results via submit(...) and ask_clarification(...) instead of done().
- Modified action logging to include action descriptions when compressLog is enabled.
- Refactored RLM configuration to support new logging and recursion features.
- Adjusted documentation to reflect changes in actor and responder interactions.
- Updated examples to demonstrate new functionality and best practices for recursive queries.
chore: release v18.0.2

feat: Enhance AxAgent with demo validation and descriptions

- Implemented validation for demos in AxProgram to ensure at least one input and one output field is present.
- Added methods `setActorDescription` and `setResponderDescription` to AxAgent for appending instructions to prompts.
- Updated documentation to reflect changes in agent identity structure and demo usage.
- Introduced tests for demo validation and description handling in AxAgent.
- Refactored prompt rendering logic to skip output-only demos.
chore: release v18.0.1

Refactor ax-llm documentation to enhance type-safe signature creation using fluent API. Update examples to demonstrate new field builders and improve clarity on usage patterns. Remove outdated string syntax examples and streamline sections on AI provider setup, agents, workflows, and error handling.
chore: release v18.0.0
Feat: Redesign of AxAgent to be RLM native

Refactor RLM inline mode: remove `resultReady` field, update tests and documentation

- Removed `resultReady` field from RLM inline mode outputs and related logic.
- Updated tests to reflect changes in output expectations.
- Revised documentation to clarify the new behavior of inline mode, emphasizing the absence of the code field as a signal for completion.
- Changed references from `AxCodeInterpreter` to `AxCodeRuntime` in documentation and code.
- Adjusted examples and usage notes to align with the new runtime model.
chore: release v17.0.11
fix: update AxJSRuntime usage instructions and enhance llmQuery handling in AxAgent
chore: release v17.0.10
feat: enhance AxJSRuntime with output mode and usage instructions
chore: release v17.0.9
feat: new inline and function modse for axagent rlm
Simplify code field execution message
add inline RLM mode defaults and sync RLM config types
chore: release v17.0.8

feat: improve error handling in AxJSRuntime and integration tests

- Enhanced error handling in the AxJSRuntime to differentiate between code errors and other errors, providing clearer error messages for SyntaxError, TypeError, ReferenceError, and RangeError.
- Updated integration tests to validate that specific error types resolve with appropriate messages instead of rejecting, ensuring better feedback for executed code.
- Refactored existing tests to improve clarity and coverage of error scenarios, including custom error handling for thrown errors.

These changes enhance the robustness of error reporting and improve the developer experience when working with the AxJSRuntime.
chore: release v17.0.7

feat: enhance error handling with data preservation in AxJSRuntime

- Added support for preserving an optional `data` field in errors thrown from the AxJSRuntime, allowing for structured error information to be maintained.
- Implemented new test cases to validate that errors retain their `data` property when caught, ensuring better context for error handling.
- Updated documentation to reflect the new `data` field in error objects, providing guidance on its usage for custom payloads.

These enhancements improve the clarity and utility of error information in the AxJSRuntime, facilitating better debugging and user experience.
chore: release v17.0.6

feat: enhance error handling in AxJSRuntime

- Introduced structured error serialization and deserialization to preserve error names, messages, and causes across worker boundaries.
- Added new test cases to validate error handling, ensuring that errors thrown in worker sessions maintain their original context when caught.
- Updated documentation to reflect changes in error handling practices for users of the code interpreter.

These improvements enhance the robustness of error management in the AxJSRuntime, providing clearer feedback and better debugging capabilities.
chore: release v17.0.5

feat: enhance RLM session management and error handling

- Updated the behavior for restarting closed RLM sessions to occur only after a timeout, improving session management.
- Modified error messages to provide clearer context on session restarts and execution timeouts.
- Added a new test case to verify that sessions do not restart without a prior timeout, ensuring expected behavior in edge cases.

These changes enhance the reliability and clarity of RLM interactions, contributing to a better user experience.
chore: release v17.0.4

feat: implement RLM session recreation and error handling

- Added functionality to recreate closed RLM sessions and retry execution with restored globals.
- Introduced a new method for session creation and improved error handling for closed session errors.
- Updated output formatting to include restart notices and handle session closure gracefully.

This enhancement ensures more robust behavior during RLM interactions, improving user experience and reliability.
chore: release v17.0.3

refactor: update RLM configuration and documentation for runtime character limits

- Renamed `maxSubQueryContextChars` to `maxRuntimeChars` for clarity and consistency, applying a shared cap for `llmQuery` context and interpreter output.
- Updated documentation to reflect the new character limits and truncation behavior.
- Enhanced examples and API references to guide users on the new configuration options and best practices for context management.
- Improved error handling in `llmQuery` to return per-item errors for batched calls and ensure proper handling of oversized contexts.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
chore: release v17.0.2

feat: rename AxCodeInterpreter to AxCodeRuntime

Adopt AxCodeRuntime as the primary RLM runtime interface while keeping a deprecated AxCodeInterpreter type alias for compatibility, and update generated exports and docs to match.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
chore: release v17.0.1

fix: make RLM interpreter returns less brittle

Allow async snippets to auto-return a safe trailing expression and support standalone sync `return <expr>` snippets so common REPL-style workflows return values reliably across calls.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
chore: release v17.0.0

feat: harden stop/abort behavior across AxGen, AxAgent, and AxFlow

Ensure stop() cancels all in-flight executions and retry backoff waits, fix retry semantics for stream termination and maxRetries boundaries, and align docs/skills with runtime naming and updated cancellation behavior.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>

feat!: rename AxJSInterpreter API to AxJSRuntime

Apply a breaking rename from AxJSInterpreter to AxJSRuntime across runtime code, exports, examples, and docs, and rename the runtime files from rlmInterpreter to jsRuntime for naming consistency.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>

readme_html:
Ax - Build Reliable AI Apps in TypeScript with DSPy

Ax brings DSPy's approach to TypeScript – describe what you want, and let the framework handle the rest. Production-ready, type-safe, works with all major LLMs.

The Problem

Building with LLMs is painful. You write prompts, test them, they break. You switch providers, everything needs rewriting. You add validation, error handling, retries – suddenly you're maintaining infrastructure instead of shipping features.

The Solution

Define what goes in and what comes out. Ax handles the rest.

import { ai, ax } from "@ax-llm/ax";

const llm = ai({ name: "openai", apiKey: process.env.OPENAI_APIKEY });

const classifier = ax(
  'review:string -> sentiment:class "positive, negative, neutral"',
);

const result = await classifier.forward(llm, {
  review: "This product is amazing!",
});

console.log(result.sentiment); // "positive"

No prompt engineering. No trial and error. Works with GPT-4, Claude, Gemini, or any LLM.

Why Ax

Write once, run anywhere. Switch between OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, or 15+ providers with one line. No rewrites.

Ship faster. Stop tweaking prompts. Define inputs and outputs. The framework generates optimal prompts automatically.

Production-ready. Built-in streaming, validation, error handling, observability. Used in production handling millions of requests.

Gets smarter. Train your programs with examples. Watch accuracy improve automatically. No ML expertise needed.

Examples

Extract structured data

const extractor = ax(`
  customerEmail:string, currentDate:datetime -> 
  priority:class "high, normal, low",
  sentiment:class "positive, negative, neutral",
  ticketNumber?:number,
  nextSteps:string[],
  estimatedResponseTime:string
`);

const result = await extractor.forward(llm, {
  customerEmail: "Order #12345 hasn't arrived. Need this resolved immediately!",
  currentDate: new Date(),
});

Complex nested objects

import { f, ax } from "@ax-llm/ax";

const productExtractor = f()
  .input("productPage", f.string())
  .output("product", f.object({
    name: f.string(),
    price: f.number(),
    specs: f.object({
      dimensions: f.object({
        width: f.number(),
        height: f.number()
      }),
      materials: f.array(f.string())
    }),
    reviews: f.array(f.object({
      rating: f.number(),
      comment: f.string()
    }))
  }))
  .build();

const generator = ax(productExtractor);
const result = await generator.forward(llm, { productPage: "..." });

// Full TypeScript inference
console.log(result.product.specs.dimensions.width);
console.log(result.product.reviews[0].comment);

Validation and constraints

const userRegistration = f()
  .input("userData", f.string())
  .output("user", f.object({
    username: f.string().min(3).max(20),
    email: f.string().email(),
    age: f.number().min(18).max(120),
    password: f.string().min(8).regex("^(?=.*[A-Za-z])(?=.*\\d)", "Must contain letter and digit"),
    bio: f.string().max(500).optional(),
    website: f.string().url().optional(),
  }))
  .build();

Available constraints: .min(n), .max(n), .email(), .url(), .date(), .datetime(), .regex(pattern, description), .optional()

Validation runs on both input and output. Automatic retry with corrections on validation errors.

Agents with tools (ReAct pattern)

const assistant = ax(
  "question:string -> answer:string",
  {
    functions: [
      { name: "getCurrentWeather", func: weatherAPI },
      { name: "searchNews", func: newsAPI },
    ],
  },
);

const result = await assistant.forward(llm, {
  question: "What's the weather in Tokyo and any news about it?",
});

AxAgent + RLM for long context

import { agent, AxJSRuntime } from "@ax-llm/ax";

const analyzer = agent(
  "context:string, query:string -> answer:string, evidence:string[]",
  {
    name: "documentAnalyzer",
    description: "Analyze very long documents with recursive code + sub-queries",
    maxSteps: 20,
    rlm: {
      contextFields: ["context"],
      runtime: new AxJSRuntime(),
      maxSubAgentCalls: 40,
      maxRuntimeChars: 2_000, // Shared cap for llmQuery context + interpreter output
      maxBatchedLlmQueryConcurrency: 6,
      subModel: "gpt-4o-mini",
    },
  },
);

const result = await analyzer.forward(llm, {
  context: veryLongDocument,
  query: "What are the main arguments and supporting evidence?",
});

RLM mode keeps long context out of the root prompt, runs iterative analysis in a persistent runtime session, and uses bounded sub-queries for semantic extraction (typically targeting <=10k chars per sub-call).

AxJSRuntime

AxJSRuntime is the built-in JavaScript runtime used by RLM and tool-style execution. It works across:

  • Node.js/Bun-style backends (worker_threads runtime path)
  • Deno backends (module worker path)
  • Browser environments (Web Worker path)

It supports:

  • Persistent sessions via createSession()
  • Function tool usage via toFunction()
  • Sandbox permissions via AxJSRuntimePermission

Multi-modal (images, audio)

const analyzer = ax(`
  image:image, question:string ->
  description:string,
  mainColors:string[],
  category:class "electronics, clothing, food, other",
  estimatedPrice:string
`);

Install

npm install @ax-llm/ax

Additional packages:

# AWS Bedrock provider
npm install @ax-llm/ax-ai-aws-bedrock

# Vercel AI SDK v5 integration
npm install @ax-llm/ax-ai-sdk-provider

# Tools: MCP stdio transport, JS runtime
npm install @ax-llm/ax-tools

Features

  • 15+ LLM Providers – OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Mistral, Ollama, and more
  • Type-safe – Full TypeScript support with auto-completion
  • Streaming – Real-time responses with validation
  • Multi-modal – Images, audio, text in the same signature
  • Optimization – Automatic prompt tuning with MiPRO, ACE, GEPA
  • Observability – OpenTelemetry tracing built-in
  • Workflows – Compose complex pipelines with AxFlow
  • RAG – Multi-hop retrieval with quality loops
  • Agents – Tools and multi-agent collaboration
  • RLM in AxAgent – Long-context analysis with recursive runtime loops
  • Zero dependencies – Lightweight, fast, reliable

Documentation

Get Started

  • Quick Start Guide – Set up in 5 minutes
  • Examples Guide – Comprehensive examples
  • DSPy Concepts – Understanding the approach
  • Signatures Guide – Type-safe signature design

Deep Dives

  • AI Providers – All providers, AWS Bedrock, Vercel AI SDK
  • AxFlow Workflows – Build complex AI systems
  • Optimization (MiPRO, ACE, GEPA) – Make programs smarter
  • AxAgent & RLM – Agents, child agents, tools, and RLM for long contexts
  • Advanced RAG – Production search and retrieval

Run Examples

OPENAI_APIKEY=your-key npm run tsx ./src/examples/[example-name].ts

Core examples: extract.ts, react.ts, agent.ts, streaming1.ts, multi-modal.ts

Production patterns: customer-support.ts, food-search.ts, rlm.ts, ace-train-inference.ts, ax-flow-enhanced-demo.ts

View all 70+ examples

Community

  • Twitter – Updates
  • Discord – Help and discussion
  • GitHub – Star the project
  • DeepWiki – AI-powered docs

Production Ready

  • Battle-tested in production
  • Stable minor versions
  • Comprehensive test coverage
  • OpenTelemetry built-in
  • TypeScript first

Contributors

  • Author: @dosco
  • GEPA and ACE optimizers: @monotykamary

License

Apache 2.0
